manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: olive, mammoth, post, grace, superhero, dance, laser, pool, spring.
Your opponent's team words are: play, cotton, unicorn, europe, bill, scale, nurse, chocolate.
Distractor words are: charge, genius, mexico, paper, pound, circle, phoenix.
The kill words are: kiwi.

message.content:
.CLUE: Agile
TARGETS: superhero, dance, spring, grace
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue relating to some of our team words. Must not be any of the listed words or contain parts of them. Must be semantically related. Provide list of targets we intend.

Team words: olive, mammoth, post, grace (as verb?), superhero, dance, laser (maybe "light"), pool, spring.

We can give a clue that relates to multiple. Possibly "water" could relate to pool, spring. Also maybe "laser" but water not related. "Ice" maybe mammoth (ice age), pool (ice pool?), spring (ice spring?), not great.

"Fossil" relates to mammoth, olive? no.

"Stage" relates to post? dance? superhero? maybe.

"Retro" none.

"Fitness" maybe dance, spring (spring training), but not others.

"Channel" no.

Perhaps "Titanic" relates to mammoth (big), post? no.

We want to maximize correct guess without hitting opponent words.

We need to avoid clue being any of opponent's words or distractors. Cannot be "play", "cotton", "unicorn", etc. Also cannot contain parts of them. So clue "water" is safe.

Water relates to pool and spring (water source). Also maybe laser? No. Could also relate to "olive"? olives are from trees that need water. Might be weak.

Maybe clue "agile"? relates to superhero (agile), dance (agile), spring (agile). Might be okay.

But we need to ensure the clue word is not part of the words (like "super" no). "Agile" not a listed word. Have to check: no opponent words contain agile.

We can list multiple targets. Suggest we go for "agile" linking superhero, dance, spring. Also maybe "grace" (graceful movement). That would be four words: superhero, dance, spring, grace. Might be ambiguous but okay.

But the clue "agile" is semantically related to those. Could also be linked to "post"? No.

We must only list those we think partner will guess. Provide clue "Agile". Provide targets: superhero, dance, spring, grace.

Check that "agile" is not containing part of any word: "agile" contains "gi" no. Not.

Ok produce answer
